title: Upon Prigg.
content:
Prigg, when he comes to houses, oft doth use,
    Rather than fail, to steal from thence old shoes:
    Sound or unsound be they, or rent or whole,
    Prigg bears away the body and the sole.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Upon Prigg."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
